programme.title: Murder in the Heartland: The Friends You Keep
programme.desc: Home will never feel safe again after Jill finds her daughter Emma Walker cold to the touch in her own bed. Quickly ruling out suicide, investigators search for a suspicious man in black caught on a neighbor's home surveillance camera.

programme.title: Murder in the Heartland: The Wishing Well Mystery
programme.desc: After Alyce Seff's body is found in a wishing well, the town of German Village, Ohio, is shocked to find the elderly landlady had as many enemies as she did friends. It will take police nine years to solve her case.

programme.title: Bride Killa: Killer Tide
programme.desc: Angelika and Vincent are the perfect couple. Both are outgoing and adventurous and they plan to celebrate their wedding on two continents with family and friends. But when a freak storm washes one of them away, sinister secrets are revealed.

programme.title: Bride Killa: Deadly Bond
programme.desc: When Miranda Dean meets Elytte Barbour it's love at first sight. Within months, they secretly marry. A dark secret comes to light shortly after the pair walks down the isle. And before long, an innocent stranger becomes the key to their wedded bliss.

programme.title: Bride Killa: Twice the Danger
programme.desc: On Kelly Ecker's wedding day she commits herself to Scott Samson, but he has other plans for his new bride. Later, Amber Fields and Josh Hilberling rush to the alter, then at each other. Both marriages start with "I do," but end in unimaginable ruin.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love to Death
programme.desc: Love and money are a beautiful marriage, for the lucky ones they create a life of happiness and ease. Unless the love is of money, the bride is a killer, and the groom their victim.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Forbidden Love
programme.desc: When sexual craving flames out of control, when desperation for love becomes all-consuming, or when illicit desire is thwarted by others some women step outside the boundaries of society. Stopping at nothing, they will kill in the name of Forbidden Love.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Hot Tempers, Cold Hearts
programme.desc: When these Deadly Women turn green-eyed they reveal their hot tempers and cold hearts.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Malicious Hearts
programme.desc: Some women have ice running through their veins. They lack the instinct to care. A mom locks up and tortures her daughter like she's a prisoner-of-war; a helpful neighbor quietly kills her community; and lesbian lovers bash and burn a complete stranger.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Loveless
programme.desc: There are many ways to leave your lover one of those ways is dead.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Jilted and Jealous
programme.desc: Jealousy can become an obsessive trait and a deadly trait.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married Today, Gone Tomorrow
programme.desc: Sometimes the most dangerous place to be is in the arms of your loved one.

programme.title: FBI Takedowns: Sadistic Mastermind
programme.desc: A sadistic criminal mastermind forces his victims to make a cruel choice: rob a bank or watch their family die. The FBI will need to take extraordinary measures to bringing this villain down before a chilling prophecy is fulfilled.

programme.title: FBI Takedowns: Catching Crazy
programme.desc: A cop-shooting fugitive terrorizes banks in South Jersey. To smoke him out, the FBI teams with a local detective and an ex-cop informant. Even with the perfect trap, this crazy criminal isn't about to go down without a fight.

programme.title: FBI Takedowns: Terrorist in Texas
programme.desc: The FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force in Dallas, TX goes undercover to stop a homegrown terrorist from killing thousands of Americans. To neutralize this violent jihadist, the FBI will have to build a weapon of mass destruction of its own design.

programme.title: Bullet Points: Showdown in the Middle East

programme.title: Bullet Points: Operation Desert Storm
programme.desc: In 1991, the United States and an unprecedented coalition of over 30 countries join forces to liberate Kuwait from invading Iraq dictator, Saddam Hussein. Highlights from the key battles, weapons and strategies that lead to a decisive victory.

programme.desc: In 1991, the United States and an unprecedented coalition of over 30 countries join forces to liberate Kuwait from invading Iraq dictator, Saddam Hussein. Highlights from the key battles, weapons and strategies that lead to a decisive victory.

programme.title: 9/11: As We Watched
programme.desc: The story of that day through the unflinching eye of ABC News, as the network's anchors and reporters rushed to the scenes of chaos and embarked on what would become an extraordinary 83 hours of live coverage.

programme.title: Bullet Points: Showdown in the Middle East
programme.desc: From 1948 to 1973 the Arabs and Israelis went to war five times for territory in the holy land of Palestine. See the key operations in one of the longest ongoing conflicts in world history.

programme.title: FBI Takedowns: Terrorist in Texas
programme.desc: The FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force in Dallas, TX goes undercover to stop a homegrown terrorist from killing thousands of Americans. To neutralize this violent jihadist, the FBI will have to build a weapon of mass destruction of its own design.

programme.title: Ancient Assassins: The Pope's Bodyguards
programme.desc: From stone-age Hawaii to 16th-century Japan, from Ancient Greece to the Byzantine Empire, there have always been elite Special Forces.

programme.title: Ancient Assassins: Beautiful Samurai
programme.desc: In 12th Century Japan, a female samurai humiliates all the others. As lethal as she was lovely, Tomoe Gozen defeated the finest swordsmen, battled tyranny and was innovative on the battlefield.

programme.title: How the World Ends: Nuclear Apocalypse
programme.desc: The Cold War might be long gone, but the nuclear threat has re-emerged. Meet nuclear preparedness enthusiasts as they dig their bunkers and stockpile supplies, determined to survive a threat that could become a global apocalypse.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: May 28, 1940, while the Wehrmacht crushes the allied armies in France, British officials prepare to face Hitler.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Franklin D. Roosevelt
programme.desc: Japanese bombers attack Pearl Harbor and sink a large part of the US Pacific Fleet. President Franklin D. Roosevelt decides it's time for the United States to strike back.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Charles de Gaulle
programme.desc: June 16, 1940. Paris has fallen to the Nazis and the German conquest of France is almost complete. Prime Minister Henri Petain plans to sign an armistice with Nazi Germany, but French General Charles de Gaulle secedes and rebels against the policy.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Adolf Hitler
programme.desc: On June 22, 1941, Adolf Hitler launches the largest military offensive in history against the Soviet Union: Operation Barbarossa. Blinded by his fire power, Hitler signs his death sentence.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Code Wars
programme.desc: American code breakers have given the United States an edge in every conflict in American history. Now, with encryption available for technological devices, U.S. code warriors have a new obstacle to manuever in exposing their enemies.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Secret Prisons
programme.desc: The dark history of America's Secret Prisons is revealed.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Past and Present
programme.desc: Many researchers believe we have been visited by otherworldly spacecraft since the dawn of man. Trace the evidence from the history of ancient Egypt, to the myths of Native Americans, and through the art of the Renaissance period.

programme.title: Ultimate Innovations in the Garden
programme.desc: Stylish outdoor decor and garden solutions.

programme.title: Home Made Easy With Mary
programme.desc: Simple cook and home ideas with host Mary DeAngelis.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Red Barn Reborn
programme.desc: Nicole takes on her most personal project yet, her grandfather's red barn. The barn has fallen into disrepair and requires a great deal of structural work to make it safe and strong.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Faulty Foundation
programme.desc: A bachelor's dark dining and living room are opened up and become one modern lounge. What was once dirty-carpeted living room with college dorm furniture and a separate ugly dining room gets completely transformed into one hip wine room.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Bugs, Birds and Bad Plumbing
programme.desc: Moving to the interior of the Dollar House, host and interior designer Nicole Curtis demolishes her heart out and focuses on the disgusting kitchen space as well as adding a half bathroom to the first floor, evicting a few pigeons along the way.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: A Mantle to Remember
programme.desc: Restoring the first floor of the Dollar House continues as host and designer Nicole Curtis uncovers hardwood floors throughout the living and dining room areas. The dining room hutch is still intact, but there is a whole new fireplace mantle to build.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Bathroom Rebuild
programme.desc: Heading up to the second floor, Nicole takes on the bathroom, one of the house's dirtiest and grimiest rooms. She brings it down to the studs, adding clean lines with white subway tile and re-purposes an old dresser into one of her signature vanities.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Floors, Floors, Floors
programme.desc: Ever closer to finishing up the interior of the Dollar House, Nicole works in the bedrooms to put up all new walls and ceilings as well as remove the black and white linoleum flooring and patch up a burn mark in the hardwood. The staircase is next.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Salvaged Back Yard
programme.desc: Moving back outside, Nicole begins to clean up all the debris from the yard and comes up with a plan to use salvaged materials from the house to create a cute little back yard area.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Total Transformation
programme.desc: All that is left to finish on the Dollar House is the exterior. The whole house needs the cedar shakes repaired and the fascia covered in aluminum for protection. Nicole looks to the neighbouring houses for inspiration as she finishes the front porch.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Underground Living
programme.desc: Specialty builders Jon and Amanda D'Aleo design and build 'Earth-sheltered' homes for clients in Southern Colorado, but they weren't expecting a project this far off the grid.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: West Coast Island Getaway
programme.desc: Building pro Mark Smith builds an off-grid modular home high up on a cliff off the coast of Vancouver, Canada. Everyone's on their toes and working as carefully as possible as even the smallest mistake could result in the home crashing down.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Island Oasis
programme.desc: Beth wants a beach getaway on the white sandy beaches of Ambergris Caye, Belize. Ken Grissom and his crew transport building materials to the remote island and construct an off-the-grid luxury getaway with the Caribbean water just steps away.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Maine Lighthouse
programme.desc: After witnessing the demise of Casco Bay's Halfway Rock lighthouse, Ford Reiche has the chance to return this icon to its former glory. It will take barges full of scaffolding, masons, and the cooperation of Mother Nature.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Taking Charge of the Yard
programme.desc: This property's unsightly guesthouse is the only thing standing in the way of its curb appeal. After removing the structure the team take care of the law.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Spacing Out
programme.desc: After taking a trip to the Kennedy Space Center, Rob gets an out-of-this-world idea for an outdoor water feature: a rocket themed splash pad! It has all the bells and whistles including water jets and lights that make this outdoor oasis perfect.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: A Hot Rod Garage
programme.desc: A great house is not complete without the ultimate garage. This project includes a TV and lounge seating for a multifunctional space that shines. New pavers and a dazzling front gate make this one garage that's hard to drive by.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Sealing The Deal
programme.desc: Every inch of this house has been fully updated, customized and improved. It's a beautiful space perfect for entertaining, relaxing, or just having fun. This home keeps it real and at the end of the day, that's what seals the deal.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Kicking Hot Pink to the Curb
programme.desc: Rob and the ninjas tackle a rundown property in an up-and-coming neighborhood in Wellington, Florida. Their budget is on a dime and they've got limited time, so they roll up their sleeves and add some VIP luxuries to transform this hot pink eyesore.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Adding Spice to the Kitchen and Living Room
programme.desc: Rob plans to spice up the kitchen and living room at his latest Wellington project by installing new bamboo flooring, appliances and real wood cabinets. The cherry on top is when the ninjas turn the dated and hated wet bar into a one-of-a-kind love seat.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: A Five-Star Master Suite
programme.desc: Rob and the ninjas take care of business by adding fresh paint and wallpaper to the master bedroom, a customized built-in closet and a wow-worthy master bath all on a tight budget.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Backyard TLC
programme.desc: The backyard at Rob's Wellington project has a ton of potential but the overgrown plants are blocking the view. By extending the patio, trimming trees and adding some TLC, the backyard is back and better than ever.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Upstairs Battle
programme.desc: Rob and the ninjas tackle a rundown property in an up-and-coming neighborhood in Wellington, Florida. The stairwell and upstairs bedrooms and bathrooms are hurtin' for certain, so Rob and the ninjas give them the VIP treatment.

programme.title: The Vanilla Ice Project: Garage Goals
programme.desc: Rob and the ninjas hit the refresh button when they upgrade the dining room and garage in the Wellington house. They dress up the walls with wallpaper and art to give these spaces a brand new start.

programme.title: Restored: 1930s Colorful Spanish Colonial Farmhouse
programme.desc: Brett restores a 1930s two-story Spanish Colonial farmhouse in Fontana, California. He learns the rich history of the home which then guides his design plan for the house exterior, entryway, living room and kitchen.

programme.title: Restored: 1915 Arts And Crafts
programme.desc: Brett Waterman gets the chance to restore an enormous Arts and Crafts home with six bedrooms for a family that grew from four children to eight children in just two years.

programme.title: Restored: 1901 Victorian Bungalow Restoration
programme.desc: Brett restores a Victorian Bungalow for a military veteran and his daughter. The project grows in scale when unexpected problems are discovered and it becomes a complete restoration.

programme.title: Restored: The Yellow Victorian Farmhouse Facelift
programme.desc: Brett restores a Victorian farmhouse with an odd floor plan. Major foundation issues complicate the project, but the happy homeowners end up with a beautiful and functional house they could never have imagined.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: All You Can Feast
programme.desc: Roger Mooking goes to Llano Seco Ranch in Chico, Calif., where he and Charlie Thieriot slow roast a 30-pound porchetta. In Sandy, Ore., Rogers meets caterers Jaret Foster and Mona Johnson and assists in roasting mussels.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: The Salt and the Sea
programme.desc: Roger Mooking is spending the day at Jacobsen Salt Co., in Netarts Bay, Oregon, one of the largest producers of handcrafted sea salt in America. Owner Ben Jacobsen takes Roger on a tour of the facility, showing him how to smoke sea salt.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Meat Me In Texas
programme.desc: Chef Roger Mooking sees that everything is bigger in Texas, first meeting Pit Master Levi Goode at Armadillo Palace in Houston. On Levi's custom rotisserie trailer, they roast a 250-pound side of beef.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: One-of-a-Kind Rigs
programme.desc: Roger meets Josh Pollack, owner of Rosenberg's Bagels & Delicatessen in Denver, Colorado.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Pork
programme.desc: Roger Mooking travels the country to discover the top fifteen greatest pork dishes.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Street Eats
programme.desc: Roger Mooking searches for the best food to eat on the fly, from food trucks and roadside stands to walk-up counters and carnival fare.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Brisket For Breakfast
programme.desc: Michael Symon visits an old San Antonio institution given new life by their phenomenal bacon and blue cheese burger.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Meat Fix Four Ways
programme.desc: Michael begins his meat-seeking journey in Lockhart, Texas, where one family remains at the forefront of America's greatest barbecue after four generations. Next, he swings over to a British-style joint in Brooklyn where they use lobster meat.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Wild Smoke and Fiery Burgers
programme.desc: In Palm Springs, Calif., Michael Symon finds a spicy burger that ignites a four-alarm fire, and a chorizo-pineapple burger sings a perfect harmony of sweet and heat.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Smoked, Stuffed and Smothered
programme.desc: In San Diego, Michael Symon devours a delicious dumpling-inspired pork and shrimp burger and an elevated pub food classic. In Shreveport, La., a wood-fired pizza is topped with spicy roast chicken and sweet corn.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Worcester, MA
programme.desc: Casey Webb lands in Worcester, MA, where he slurps down some radical ramen, indulges in peanut butter cup French toast and takes on a rack of deep-fried ribs with the heat of a million Scoville units.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation: Baltimore, MD
programme.desc: Casey Webb's epic eating expedition brings him to Baltimore for an out-of-this-world diner, a classic crab feast and a four-pound pho fight.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hoboken, New Jersey
programme.desc: Casey Webb's appetizing adventures take him to Hoboken, New Jersey, New York's neighbor to the west and the home of the great Frank Sinatra. He munches on homemade mozzarella and takes a bite out of a bagel behemoth.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Sacramento, California
programme.desc: Casey Webb swings into Sacramento, California, for three gold-standard meals from the Golden State, including a perfectly fried Chinese-American chicken, a savory multi-meat waffle stack.

programme.desc: Will and Hilary interview a dismissed juror from Philip's case on Hilary's show.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Not I Barbecue
programme.desc: Will and Carlton get caught in a compromising position with two sexy intellectuals.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Not With My Cousin You Don't

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Viva Lost Wages
programme.desc: When Will and Carlton go to Las Vegas, one of them gambles away their tickets home.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: There's the Rub (Part 1)

programme.title: Movie: Juice
programme.desc: Four Harlem teens are out skipping school one day when they find out an old friend was killed in a shootout at a bar. After the horrific incident, Bishop tells his friends that they have no respect, no juice. To appear 'street,' they rob a corner store.

programme.title: Movie: All Eyez on Me
programme.desc: The true story of musician Tupac Shakur's life. Raised by activists, the young black man grew up to be a poet, actor and one of the world's most successful rappers whose impact has only grown since his death, turning him into a global cultural icon.

programme.title: Movie: Love Under the Rainbow
programme.desc: A woman has dreamt her whole life about finding her true love and starting a family. Now, she's a teacher who is still on her quest for love. When a new architect moves to town, romance is in the air for both of them.

programme.title: Movie: Valentine Ever After
programme.desc: A young widowed father that has an adolescent son helps him navigate love and discovers he has a lot to learn himself.

programme.title: Movie: Love, Romance and Chocolate
programme.desc: A Manhattan chocolatier heads to Belgium with plans to close up her family's chocolate shop, but gets swept up in a competition to become the Belgian Royal family's chocolatier. The potential for love might be the only thing to shake her focus.

programme.title: Movie: Matching Hearts
programme.desc: As Valentine's Day nears, a matchmaker is tasked by her mentor to find a match for an entrepreneur who believes staying single is the key to success.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Triple Play
programme.desc: Miles introduces Rose to his daughter Caroline; Blanche rents a Mercedes to attract men.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Clinton Avenue Memoirs

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Like the Beep Beep of the Tom-Tom
programme.desc: Blanche knows she needs a pacemaker, but she fears it'll halt her sex life.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: An Illegitimate Concern

programme.title: The Golden Girls: 72 Hours

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Twice in a Lifetime
programme.desc: Rose briefly rekindles a romance she had with Buzz Mueller, but realizes she loves Miles.

programme.title: The Closer: Fool's Gold
programme.desc: Provenza gets trapped into helping his first ex-wife recover her wedding ring after she got ripped off by a shady gold buyer. He once again finds himself in a professionally embarrassing situation.

programme.title: The Closer: Drug Fiend
programme.desc: In a case that hits too close to home for Brenda, a well-liked cancer doctor is brutally killed and his drug supply wiped clean. To make matters worse, Captain Raydor has a new assignment that leaves Brenda questioning her loyalty to Pope.

programme.title: The Closer: Last Rites
programme.desc: The murder of a priest puts the squad on a collision course with the Catholic Church and drives a wedge between Brenda and Chief Pope. Meanwhile, Brenda's parents are in town while her father continues his cancer treatments.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Let the Angels Commit
programme.desc: Much to the envy of her fellow doctors, Cristina scrubs in on the rare 'humpty dumpty' procedure before Alex questions his future medical specialty. Derek gets a surprise while George and Addison work with a pregnant woman with an unusual dilemma.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Where the Boys Are
programme.desc: While Addison and Callie team up to work on a patient, the boys of Seattle Grace go on a camping trip together. A suspicious Bailey confronts Cristina while Meredith assists Sloan with a procedure unlike any she has ever experienced at the hospital.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Staring at the Sun
programme.desc: The O'Malley family visits George at the hospital while Richard endeavours to reunite with his wife. Meanwhile, Derek and Addison strive to achieve a civil relationship as Meredith's determination leads her to have a more positive outlook on life.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Affair Trade
programme.desc: Doug panics when he starts receiving sexy phone calls from a woman claiming to have spent a weekend in the Hamptons with him. Meanwhile, Danny attempts to sabotage a potential relationship for Spence when he fears losing his best friend to a woman.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Major Disturbance
programme.desc: While reading a bedtime story to Deacon's son, Doug changes the ending to make it a little scarier and ends up traumatizing the boy. Meanwhile, Arthur offers himself up as a passenger for hire so people can ride in the carpool lane.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Moxie Moron
programme.desc: Doug takes on a supervisory role at IPS just to prove to Carrie that he's not content with being a driver for the rest of his life. Meanwhile, Carrie decides to show her bosses that she has moxie by complaining about the company holiday gift.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Ruff Goin'
programme.desc: When Doug and Carrie are not invited to their block party, Doug makes an appearance to prove they are good people, but ends up having to adopt a dog from his neighbour in order to prove it. Meanwhile, Arthur offers to help Danny with his gambling problem.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Frozen in Time
programme.desc: A young women went missing for three years until someone called to report a suspicious truck in a driveway. The cold trail turned even colder when they found out what was being stored in the truck.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Cold Storage
programme.desc: Tracy Jo Shine grew up in a middle-class home in Houston, Texas. Unfortunately, when she attended a prestigious high school she began experimenting with drugs. When she went missing, her family suspected her new friends from school.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Broken Bond
programme.desc: The story of nurse Tanya Reid who may have murdered her own baby and was planning to murder her son.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Out of the Ashes
programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend’s house. She was never seen or heard from again.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Missing Pearl
programme.desc: A woman doesn't return home, but a trail of blood is discovered leading to a basement.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Man's Best Friend?
programme.desc: When a young girl was found dead, her parents told police that she was killed by a dog.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hand Delivered
programme.desc: An anonymous 'tip' referring to unpublicized aspects of an unsolved case, leads police on a hunt for a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Death Play
programme.desc: Marie Robard's character in her high school's play 'Hamlet' hinted at her own inner turmoil.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Disappearance of Helle Crafts
programme.desc: A flight attendant was reported missing from her home after hiring an investigator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Footpath Murders
programme.desc: British detectives teamed up with a pioneering scientist to crack a case of sexual assault and serial murder. This 1986 case marked the first time DNA was used as evidence in a court of law.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Planted Evidence
programme.desc: A woman is found beaten, bound, strangled and possibly raped outside of Phoenix. The nearby plants tell investigators more about the killer than any other single piece of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Southside Strangler
programme.desc: Learn how investigators caught Timothy Wilson Spencer, better known as the Southside Strangler, a serial killer responsible for the murders of five women in Virginia. This was the first use of DNA profiling in the U.S. justice system.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Legionnaires' Disease
programme.desc: History was made in Philadelphia in the summer of 1976 -- not because of the bicentennial, but because of the mysterious death of 34 Legionnaires.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Wilson Murder
programme.desc: An eye doctor is found beaten to death, by his wife. When police arrest an itinerant painter for the murder, he accuses the wife and her sister of hiring him to kill the doctor. Investigators must use forensic science to work out just what happen.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The List Murders
programme.desc: For 18 years, a man who murdered his entire family successfully eluded the FBI. Investigators used art and forensic science to find the killer and bring him to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Raw Terror
programme.desc: The story of Damion Heersink, an eleven-year-old boy who almost died after eating an improperly cooked hamburger teeming with E-Coli, and the people who saved his life.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Common Thread
programme.desc: The year was 1984, and a serial killer was on the loose in Florida. Eight women had been found dead. At each crime scene, investigators found tiny red fibers, fibers they hoped would lead them to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Dirty Deed
programme.desc: Eileen and Derrick Severs disappeared from their home in the small village of Hambleton in Great Britain, and police found evidence which suggested foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Killer Fog
programme.desc: Investigators set out to determine if the dense, thick fog that caused the deadliest automobile accident on American history was a natural phenomenon, or the result of something else.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sex, Lies and DNA
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The search for Ruby Morris involved dozens of investigators and scientists, even the coast guard.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bitter Potion
programme.desc: When a woman comes down with flu-like symptoms and eventually dies, a neurologist suspects she was poisoned with radioactive thallium. The FBI suspects her neighbour, who had a very extensive background in chemistry.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Blood Trail
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb’s head along with a note which read, 'You next.'

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fatal Fungus
programme.desc: In Cleveland, more than thirty cases of people suffering from bleeding lungs were reported in the span of a few months. Doctors had never seen anything like it, and searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Something's Fishy
programme.desc: Examining the cyanide-laced Excedrin killings of 1986, where two people died. When the killer was caught, theirs was the first conviction after federal laws against product tampering were instituted after the Chicago Tylenol murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sealed With a Kiss
programme.desc: For more than a year, angry, hateful letters were sent to a first grade school teacher in a small town in Pennsylvania. When scientists analyzed the letters, they found evidence that the sender knew a lot about the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Postal Mortem
programme.desc: On October 15, 1985, two bombs rocked Salt Lake City and resulted in two deaths. A third explosion occurred the next day, but this time the victim survived. Police believed that the survivor is more than an innocent by-stander.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Micro-Clues
programme.desc: When a boy is found dead in a cornfield, traces of water from his body help lead investigators to the boy's killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Parasites
programme.desc: In the spring of 1993, an unexplained illness struck the residents of Milwaukee, Wisconsin. Health officials suspected it was influenza, but it proved to be more serious and more difficult to identify.

programme.desc: A daily dose of news, weather, sports, entertainment, finance and travel, in a fast paced, approachable style.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Without a Trace
programme.desc: The deaths of a child and a truck driver in the same hospital on the same day are investigated to see if there is a link between them.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Knot for Everyone
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, bodies of nude woman were found in remote hills of California. The police got a break when a known prostitute escaped an attacker and was able to identify him and his automobile. The suspect was 50-year-old Roger Kibbe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Profiles of intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world are examined.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Grave Evidence
programme.desc: The shooting of a man during a hunting trip is reopened years after the incident, and the victim's hunting partner becomes a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Profiles of intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world are examined.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Siberia's Coldest Case
programme.desc: Josh Gates concludes his in-depth investigation of the Dyatlov Pass Incident. Taking on Dead Mountain's brutal conditions, Josh and two investigators re-trace the last steps of the hikers. A big discovery could finally solve the mystery.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: America's Lost WWII Hero
programme.desc: Josh Gates explores the case of lost World War II hero Gertrude 'Tommy' Tompkins of the Women Air Force Service Pilots, who went missing when her P-51 Mustang crashed. Josh joins search teams using cutting-edge technology to bring the lost hero home.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Unearthed: Digging Into D-Day: Part 1
programme.desc: Josh Gates touches down in Normandy to investigate battles on land, air and sea. The expedition reveals a Nazi threat greater than ever imagined.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Unearthed: Digging Into D-Day Part 2
programme.desc: Josh's expedition at Normandy reveals a Nazi threat greater than ever imagined, unearthing a massive German bunker complex buried for 75 years.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: America's Vanished Airliner
programme.desc: Josh Gates investigates the most mysterious airline disaster in American history, searching for why Northwest Flight 2501 vanished taking the lives of all 58 people on-board. Later, Josh and his team examine sightings of the Mothman across the Midwest.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Code to Gold
programme.desc: Josh scours Virginia backcountry in search of the legendary Thomas Beale Treasure, worth millions today. With encrypted codes hiding its whereabouts, Josh takes a detour to Philadelphia to uncover missing pieces to the puzzle.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: America's Vanished Airliner
programme.desc: Josh Gates investigates the most mysterious airline disaster in American history, searching for why Northwest Flight 2501 vanished taking the lives of all 58 people on-board. Later, Josh and his team examine sightings of the Mothman across the Midwest.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: The Real Robin Hood
programme.desc: Josh criss-crosses the English countryside in search of the real Robin Hood.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Global Game Show: Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Josh plays a hilarious traveling trivia game in Greece. This episode features behind the scenes extras, expanded scenes, and highlights from his investigation into the lost civilization of the Minoans.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Global Game Show: Without Borders
programme.desc: Josh quizzes fellow travelers as he explores the globe, and anyone, anywhere could be his next contestant. Josh also shares highlights, behind the scenes extras, and never before televised footage from some of his most recent adventures.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Downed and Out, Part II
programme.desc: Marooned on a barren island under the tropical sun, Matt and Joe risk a long swim through shark-infested waters and fight for life-sustaining basics at every turn.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Coastal Catastrophe
programme.desc: Marooned on a rocky coast in the Pacific Northwest, Matt and Joe struggle to get away from powerful waves and come head to head over the question of whether to hunker down or travel deeper into one of the darkest, wettest forest in the United States.

programme.title: Dual Survival: No End in Sight
programme.desc: Stranded in a mangrove swamp in Sri Lanka, Joe and Cody escape the crocodile-filled waters only to find themselves in a leech-infested jungle. But this time, distressed elephants interrupt their journey back and they may not get out alive.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Swamplandia
programme.desc: Stranded in a black water swamp in southern Georgia, Joe and Matt are deep in alligator territory where peat bogs, mysterious floating islands, and poisonous snakes stand in the way of getting out and finding rescue.

programme.title: Dual Survival: No End in Sight
programme.desc: Joe comes face-to-face with elephants and is forced off a cliff, Cody masters the ancient fire piston, and they cross a crocodile-infested lake to escape the longest survival scenario in history of 'Dual Survival.'

programme.title: Dual Survival: Grin and Bear It
programme.desc: Joe and Matt trek through the Alleghany Mountains of Pennsylvania, where Joe grew-up and first learned survival skills. Traveling in freezing rain, blocked by steep ravines, and deep in bear country, they're racing against Mother Nature to get out alive.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Journey's End to a New Beginning
programme.desc: After 15 episodes, the partnership of Cody Lundin and Joe Teti is coming to an end. This special episode goes behind the scenes with the show's crew to get the scoop on who is leaving and who's moving on with a new partner.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Into the Canyons
programme.desc: Joe and Matt face down the high desert canyons of Matt's home of 20 years: southern Utah. Without knives or any modern supplies, they attempt to survive and seek rescue in this harsh terrain that's more favorable to rattlesnakes and mountain lions.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Waterlogged
programme.desc: Off the beaten path and waterlogged in the rainforest of Costa Rica, Matt and Joe battle torrential rains, deadly insects, and raging rapids. After missing a shot at rescue, they combine survival ingenuity with indigenous skills to get out alive.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue: Wisconsin Washout
programme.desc: Marty takes on a huge diverter trench to deflect torrents of storm water from devasting the homestead of musician Lou and his girlfriend Stacy. But a storm is the forecast, so no one can predict if this rescue will succeed.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Lacey's Story
programme.desc: Lacey ballooned up over 600 lbs on pizza and ice cream and knows Dr. Now is her best hope for success. She must now rally her divorced parents around her for support and find a way to modulate her moods that won't include binge-eating.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Travis' Story
programme.desc: When Travis got too big to walk down the aisle, he promised to give his wife a proper wedding one day. Since then he's only gained weight. Now, Dr. Now is Travis' only hope to lose weight and get the life he and his wife dreamed of having together.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Joyce's Story
programme.desc: Joyce needs home health care 24/7 and hasn't left the house in 3 years. But getting to Dr. Now in Houston is just the first step, and when he prescribes therapy where Joyce must revisit an unhappy past with her mom, it might be more than she can handle.

programme.title: My Feet Are Killing Me: ET Fingers
programme.desc: Dr. Vincent tries to save a man's enormous toe, as Dr. Schaeffer has his hands full treating a little boy with big demands, and a man with incredibly long toes.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: Twenty of Everything
programme.desc: After 30 years of hoarding, Gary's house is so full that he is forced to sleep outside on a makeshift bed.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: Twenty of Everything
programme.desc: After 30 years of hoarding, Gary's house is so full that he is forced to sleep outside on a makeshift bed.

programme.desc: It's Christmas and the family is divided over Kody's pitch to live in one house. There's a big surprise when a pregnancy is revealed. Later the family confronts the fact that their houses in Vegas are not selling. Their finances are at a breaking point.

programme.title: I Didn't Know I Was Pregnant: Surprise Guest

programme.title: I Didn't Know I Was Pregnant: Baby by the Book

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: A Horrible Sight
programme.desc: Rick tries to salvage his cluttered home that was nearly destroyed in a house fire.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: My Biggest Embarrassment

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Joseph and Sabrina
programme.desc: Joseph's been sending sexy Sabrina his love and his money but his sister doesn't buy Sabrina's lies and calls in Nev and Kamie for some truth. What they discover is so shocking that they wonder if Sabrina's lies will ever end.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Live Punishment Special
programme.desc: The guys celebrate 100 episodes of Impractical Jokes with a live punishment in the heart of NYC. Each Joker will walk a tightrope five stories high.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Big Uneasy
programme.desc: The guys take over New Orleans, as they steal sips from strangers' drinks at a famous bar, spin the Wheel of Voo-Doom on Bourbon Street, and crash some horse and buggy rides. Then, tonight's loser turns into a legendary swamp monster.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hopeless and Changeless
programme.desc: The guys head to the park asking strangers to agree with their bizarre points of view, then give classless lessons about real estate. Plus, tonight's loser finds himself in a harrowing race to the finish at the racetrack.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Autograph Corrector
programme.desc: The guys rely on the kindness of strangers to help answer some awkward questions, then go looking for love in the park. Plus, tonight's big loser strikes out amongst baseball fans with an assist from Yankees legend Tino Martinez.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Bogey Man
programme.desc: The laughing gas flows freely when the guys pose as dental assistants. Later, they make headlines for all the wrong reasons, and get burned giving some campfire tips. Plus, tonight’s big loser scores a hole in none on the links.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: The guys try and work out the kinks as physical therapists, then pitch some way out-of-the-box ideas for TV hosts. Plus, the big loser gets rolled over by some speedy ladies.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Pantsing With the Stars
programme.desc: The guys pair up to share educational ideas with perplexed parents, then teach Krav Maga to martial arts newbies. When one joker refuses, he becomes the big loser, and faces an opponent that scares the pants off him.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Staten Island Holiday Spectacular
programme.desc: The guys celebrate with some unseasonable specials, followed by new toys you may not want under the tree. Then, gift wrapping goes awry, and the big loser has a close encounter with some hungry wild life.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Well...
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr play a game of tag at a coffee shop, then convince shoppers to deliver sketchy messages at the grocery store. Plus, tonight's big loser might lose his mind in Impractical Jokers' first-ever non-punishment punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Prize Fighter
programme.desc: The guys get their checks bounced by customers who notice unusual charges, then help out coworkers with some peculiar trust exercises. Later, the big loser finds himself deep at a carnival game that's rigged for him to lose.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Sun-Fan Lotion
programme.desc: Murr, Q and Sal are at the massage parlor for much kneaded relaxation, and then ask strangers their opinions on questionable yearbook ads. Later, tonight's big loser has an awkward but fan-tastic time in the sun.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Q-Pay
programme.desc: The guys kill time in a waiting room by sharing some TMI texts with strangers, then try to sabotage each other as the wingmen from hell at a dating event. Tonight's big loser gets a very special donation from one of the other Jokers.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr cruise the food court sending texts to strangers, then throw caution, and groceries, to the wind. And tonight's loser is cast as a genie in a play but wishes he could be anywhere else.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr explain why they've been doused with water at the mall, and tonight's big loser plays a pirate but won't find any rum in his cup.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q beg shoppers to hold on to a weird item, then teach tech to seniors. Tonight's loser reveals his embarrassing digital life to his dad.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Training Day
programme.desc: The guys go to a supermarket with bells on and try to reminisce with strangers in the park. Plus, tonight's three losers are put through an epic trial by fire.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stage Fright
programme.desc: The guys serve up raw deals at a pawn shop counter and try to convince strangers to give them hugs. Plus, tonight's loser performs his life story through song.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Spider Man
programme.desc: The guys give all-you-can-eat buffet customers all they can handle, and turn into real power tools while teaching workshops at a hardware store. Then, tonight's big loser suffers a punishment that will make your skin crawl.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Nitro Circus Spectacular
programme.desc: A live, two-hour event, featuring obstacle courses, high-flying jumps, and a punishment unlike anything you've seen before.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Wrapper's Delight
programme.desc: The guys make a mockery of each other while acting as restaurant managers, sing along with strangers trying to name that (made up) tune. The loser gets wrapped up in a birthday surprise that definitely wasn't on his wish list.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Chairman
programme.desc: The guys shock their fellow shoppers by going footloose and fancy free in a discount store, then pose insane questions to strangers in the park. Tonight's loser is taken for the ride of his life in a tricked out wheelchair he can't possibly control.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stripped of Dignity
programme.desc: The guys get a taste of babysitting while asking reluctant strangers to watch their kid, then head to the grocery store to conduct taste(less) tests with shoppers, and tonight's loser is forced to strip for the wrong people.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Doomed
programme.desc: Joe, Q, Sal and Murr take in more than they bargained for while working at a tailor shop, then convince strangers to hand over their cellphones. Plus, tonight's big loser sacrifices his childhood for a punishment three months in the making.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Charity Case

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Theater del Absurdo
programme.desc: The guys raise money for absurd charities at a donut shop, join diners mid-meal in a Chinese restaurant, and offer unsolicited advice to shoppers in a mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Everything's Just Rosie
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q, and Murr strike out with patrons in a bowling alley and try to force-feed unsuspecting diners. Plus, a surprise visit by Rosie O’Donnell has tonight’s loser seeing double.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Enter the Dragons

programme.desc: French fries are the topic of competition in a food court. The gang then starts trouble in a Burger restaurant.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Sorry for Your Loss

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: All the Wrong Moves
programme.desc: The guys blindly follow each other's humiliating directions at a water park, compete to sell their ridiculous book ideas, and try to convince unknowing strangers to save their seats in Time Square.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Chock Full of Chocolate
programme.desc: It's a chocolate lovers' delight with Marcela Valladolid's decadent chocolate devil's food cake and new ways to make delicious treats with chocolate bars. Use instant pudding to make Jeff Mauro's creme brulee and Katie Lee's chocolate cherry pudding pops.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Have the Best Birthday
programme.desc: Katie Lee makes porterhouse steak with herb fries. Geoffrey Zakarian makes shrimp bruschetta, and Sunny Anderson makes mini pita pizzas.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Munch Madness
programme.desc: The hosts go head-to-head armed with regional dishes in a basketball-inspired 'munch madness' tournament. Jeff represents the Midwest with his Chicago-style Italian beef sandwich while Katie Lee shares a Southern fried bologna sandwich.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Shanghai
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern introduces the popular tastes of Shanghai, China. From creamy hairy crab and savory pork-filled dumplings to rice wine-soaked chicken and scallion pancakes on the go, cuisine in this big city is sweet and simple.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Jerusalem
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern investigates the rich culinary heritage of Jerusalem. From savory classics like shawarma, falafel, kubbeh and hummus to tempting pastries like halva and bureka, this holy city's edible icons are steeped in history.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Dallas
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern explores the big and bold flavors that are decidedly Dallas. From comforting chicken fried steak and sweet, creamy pecan pie to hearty tortilla soup and steak aged for 240 days.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Cape Town
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern leaves no meal unexplored in multicultural Cape Town, South Africa, where meat comes in all forms, from sausage to stew to pie. The coastal city's seafood is out of this world, and the favorite pastry is nothing without a coat of syrup!

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Asheville
programme.desc: Andrew reveals the iconic eats of Asheville, North Carolina. The eclectic food city features tangy barbecue, decadent sweet potato pie, mouth-watering fried chicken, succulent rainbow trout and giant soft-dough biscuits with savory gravy.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Mumbai
programme.desc: Andrew unveils Mumbai's multicultural flavors and addictive ingredients. A city built on chaos and hard work, Mumbai showcases its history through its delicious eateries. The city's edible icons include deep-fried fish and prawns and saucy goat.

programme.title: Jessie: Help Not Wanted

programme.title: Raven's Home: Dream Moms

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: Who Let the Dogs In
programme.desc: Sydney adopts a dog behind her dad's back, but has trouble hiding it. Young Max gets a turtle as a 'training pet' to prove his responsibility.

programme.title: Coop & Cami Ask the World: Would You Wrather Have a Frozen Phone?
programme.desc: Cooper is nervous about his upcoming Junior Firefighters test, but everyone assures him that he’ll pass because his dad was a firefighter.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Grandma-A-Rooney

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Prom-a-Rooney

programme.title: Raven's Home: Sleevemore Part One: Frozen

programme.desc: Cesar helps actress Rhona Mitra curb her bulldog Oscar's violent tendencies. Later, newlyweds Lisa and Paul hope Cesar can sort out the canine conundrum that has cut their honeymoon short.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Cesar, We Have a Problem
programme.desc: For real-life astronauts Suni and Leland, space travel is a breeze compared to getting their dogs to play nice. Then, Cesar meets Mongo, a pit-mix doubling as an escape artist.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Shadow Dogs
programme.desc: Responsible for handling search and rescue dogs, Marcy and Nick know that canine obedience can mean the difference between life and death. But German shepherd pups Neco and Yona have a shadowy track record.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Udder Madness
programme.desc: Featuring emergencies with Dr. Pol and his staff.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Deja MOO!: Rock 'N Pol
programme.desc: Dr. Pol and Charles must solve a mystery as ten Angus steers have suddenly gone lame and cannot stand.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Deja MOO!: Polnado Warning
programme.desc: Dr. Pol is busy with patients when the tornado sirens start blaring. The whole clinic, including pets and owners must head to the basement for their safety and ride out the storm.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Deja MOO!: Red, White and Moo
programme.desc: We head back to the Fourth of July. Before Dr. Pol and his staff are able to fully enjoy the celebrations, they must take care of animals that need their help immediately.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: Crash of the Concorde
programme.desc: The Concorde was the supersonic passenger plane that could fly from New York to London in under four hours, but after a fatal accident kills 100, experts reveal a secret history of the iconic aircraft that led to this devastating explosion.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: Cruise Ship Catastrophe
programme.desc: When a cruise ship meets disaster, the ship begins to sink and the captain flees, leaving 300 passengers stranded; now, new discoveries reveal the fatal truth behind the devastating wreck that claimed 32 lives.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: When Katrina Struck
programme.desc: Hurricane Katrina is one of the worst natural disasters to ever strike American soil, but as the storm revealed widespread construction failures, it's a tragedy of engineering, too; the latest science reveals how.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes: Olympic Stadium Fiasco
programme.desc: Montreal's Olympic Stadium was an ambitious engineering project with a striking Space Age design, but it's distinctive roof had caused over one billion dollars in cost overruns. Experts reveal what went wrong and how cutting-edge science can fix it.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes: Boston's Big Dig Disaster
programme.desc: Boston's Big Dig was one of the most ambitious engineering projects in history, but in 2006, a fatal collapse in one of its tunnels put the ambitious project in doubt; experts reveal what caused this engineering disaster and how it was fixed.

programme.title: Impossible Engineering: World's Highest Bridge
programme.desc: The world's highest bridge impossibly spans an 1,800-foot chasm known as the Crack in the Earth, and to build this engineering marvel, experts use cutting-edge construction technology that can conquer the deadly cliffs of this rugged environment.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Big: Volkswagen Mega Factory
programme.desc: Richard visits the biggest car factory in the globe, located in Wolfsburg Germany. A space with the kind of technology and pace that allows for the churning out of one new car every 16 seconds.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes: Curse of the Devil's Dam
programme.desc: When a dam breaks during routine maintenance, engineers fear a catastrophic flood. Experts use the latest science to investigate what went wrong and how a simple miscalculation could be to blame.

programme.title: Impossible Engineering: World's Highest Bridge
programme.desc: The world's highest bridge impossibly spans an 1,800-foot chasm known as the Crack in the Earth, and to build this engineering marvel, experts use cutting-edge construction technology that can conquer the deadly cliffs of this rugged environment.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Atlantis: The New Mystery
programme.desc: New evidence reveals that Atlantis is not just a myth, but a real civilization devastated by an apocalyptic volcanic eruption, and now, experts use the latest science to unearth discoveries that might confirm the location of this mysterious lost city.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Stonehenge: The Forbidden History
programme.desc: Experts use the latest technology to reveal how a primitive civilization built one of the world's greatest structures, Stonehenge.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Secrets of the Alien Relic
programme.desc: A strange, alien artifact discovered in the ruins of an ancient Greek shipwreck holds the secrets of a technology thousands of years ahead of its time. Now experts are using cutting-edge science to reveal what this mysterious device was used for.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Hunt for the Ark of the Covenant
Secrets of the Lost: Egypt's Atlantis: The New Treasures
programme.desc: After experts discover an ancient lost city off the coast of Egypt, the race to unearth its priceless relics and bring this mysterious sunken world back to life begins, revealing the doomsday event that caused it to sink into the sea.

programme.title: Curse of the Bermuda Triangle: Vanishing of Flight 19
programme.desc: Five US Navy planes vanish without a trace in the Bermuda Triangle, fueling speculation that this strange stretch of water is cursed. Using the latest science and tech, a team of experts investigate why the planes disappeared and where they are today.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Stonehenge of the Holy Land
programme.desc: Legends of ancient giants surround a mysterious monument in the Holy Land. A bright pink lake could point to a strange, undiscovered new species. When a young boy disappears in a sand dune, satellite images help with the investigation.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Dracula's Tomb
programme.desc: Strange mounds in Transylvania might pinpoint the location of the tomb of the bloodthirsty tyrant believed to have inspired Dracula. A mysterious ancient city is discovered on a coral reef. Images of a plane crash sets off a hunt for a drug lord.

programme.title: What on Earth?: North Korea's Forbidden Islands
programme.desc: Man-made islands are spotted near a North Korean missile facility. When something unexpected is found in a remote desert, experts investigate a lost city of giants. A strange discovery in Siberia could reveal a secret mission to the center of the earth.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Mystery of the Desert Shipwrecks
programme.desc: Hundreds of shipwrecks are found in the middle of a desert in Africa. Then, visit a ghost town where hundreds of its inhabitants have mysteriously died. Also, something strange happens in North Korea.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Lost City of Gold
programme.desc: Weird barren rings in the Namib Desert, an island the size of Manhattan that vanishes without trace and a lost city in the heart of the Amazonian jungle. What on Earth? Investigates six more mysteries captured from space.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ghost Ship of Hell Island
programme.desc: A ghost ship is found abandoned on a remote island that's home to one of the world's last uncontacted tribes. A strange labyrinth in Sicily might have a sinister connection to the Mafia. New evidence reveals a mysterious military compound in Russia.

programme.title: Marcia Clark Investigates The First 48: Robert Blake
programme.desc: Marcia Clark attempts to uncover the truth behind the unsolved murder of actor Robert Blake's wife, Bonnie Lee Bakley. Determined to find Bakley’s killer, Marcia scrutinizes this twisted Hollywood tale to separate the facts from the fiction.

programme.title: The First 48: The Detective Speaks: Deadly Morning
programme.desc: In Cleveland, a young mother walking alone to work is viscously murdered and dumped in an abandoned house.

programme.title: The First 48: Missing Persons: Critical Care
programme.desc: When a caretaker reports her 63-year-old cancer patient missing, detectives research leads to solve the mystery.

programme.title: The First 48: The Detective Speaks: Murder in Treme

programme.title: Nightwatch: Katrina
programme.desc: On the tenth anniversary of Hurricane Katrina, everyone in New Orleans and on the Nightwatch recall their experiences with the storm, and reflect upon the significance it still holds in their lives.

programme.desc: Detectives are determined to take down Quack, one of Laredo's most notorious heroin dealers.

programme.title: Bordertown: Laredo: A River Runs Through It
programme.desc: A woman drives with 60 pounds of dope in her car trunk while her daughter sleeps in the backseat.

programme.title: Bordertown: Laredo: A Needle in a Haystack
programme.desc: With a large shipment of heroin about to arrive in Laredo, the LPD team conducts a citywide crackdown of suspected heroin dealers to learn where and when the drugs will arrive.

programme.title: Bordertown: Laredo: Fifty Tons
programme.desc: A local casino's connection to a prostitution ring may give detectives the chance to hurt the cartels.

programme.title: Crime 360: Deadly Secrets
programme.desc: In Cleveland, Ohio, lead homicide detective Wally Everett and his partner Mike Smith are called out to a crime scene where a body lays hidden between some hedges and the victim's grandmother's house.

programme.title: Deadly Women: The Takers
programme.desc: All of us would like a little extra cash, but only a few are prepared to steal a life to get it.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Beauty and the Beastly

programme.desc: Commitments’ out clause is 'til death do us part.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Kill the Competition
programme.desc: In the game of love the stakes are high not everyone can be a winner.

programme.desc: Don Fluitt is dead. Now, body-cam video and original audio capture the detectives hunt for his killer. As the case unravels, security video captures the moments just before the evil deed and a surprising twist will bring this killer to their knees.

programme.title: The Murder Tapes: Puzzle Piece
programme.desc: When the battered body of Bianca Rainer, mother of two young girls, is found in rural Arkansas, the police have just one unusual clue, a puzzle piece found stuck to her body. But they know if they find the missing puzzle, they will find her murderer.

programme.title: In Pursuit with John Walsh: The Criminal Caregiver
programme.desc: After a vulnerable elderly man is brutalized in his rural home, John joins a thrilling manhunt for Derek Whisenand. And when newlywed bliss turns into a vicious crime, Cal goes boots on the ground on the trail of fugitive Arnoldo Jimenez.

programme.title: In Pursuit with John Walsh: When Good Meets Evil
programme.desc: John profiles Juan Lazaro Abrego, a man believed to have killed his ex-girlfriend, while Callahan investigates Derek Dean, a man accused of shooting a pair of identical twins, killing one and wounding the other.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: The Grim Sleeper
programme.desc: A lone survivor is the only witness who can help police track down a serial killer known as the Grim Sleeper, who preys on women in South Central Los Angeles. After decades of searching, police hone in on an unlikely suspect.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Murder Among Friends
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, a popular teen goes for a run around her hometown in Maine. Days later, her savagely beaten body is found. But what happened remains a mystery for years, until her mother intervenes and forces police to re-examine the case.

programme.title: Wives With Knives: Shear Rage
programme.desc: Stories of women who stab their significant others.

programme.title: Wives With Knives: Family Ties
programme.desc: A young woman scarred by childhood traumas struggles to rebuild her life. She meets the man of her dreams but once they're engaged the couple's relationship crumbles. It's a dangerous game of he said she said that finally comes to a bloody end.

programme.title: Wives With Knives: The Quiet Storm
programme.desc: Teenagers Tamara and Leo are forced to marry when Tamara gets pregnant but the strain of raising a family rips them apart. Tamara falls for a childhood friend, Marvin but the romance turns ugly when he starts abusing her and she fights back with a knife.

programme.title: Married With Secrets: Two-Faced
programme.desc: The decomposed body of a San Francisco businessman is found floating in the California Aqueduct. Investigators initially suspect a family member, but without evidence, the case goes cold. It will take detectives several decades to find the real killer.

programme.title: Married With Secrets: Cold Blue Blood
programme.desc: Dr. Dirk Greineder is one of the most respected men in the upper class community of Wellesley, Massachusetts… that is, until a secret X-rated addiction spirals out of control and results in a gruesome murder no one saw coming.

programme.title: Married With Secrets: Dancing with Death
programme.desc: Wife and mother of three Stephanie Roller Bruner has a wonderful life, so her disappearance days before Thanksgiving leads to a massive search. When information of a secret love triangle comes out, the case takes a drastic turn.

programme.title: Married With Secrets: Serial Deception
programme.desc: When dozens of women vanish from the sunshine state in the late 60s, they're presumed runways. It isn't until police discover the bodies of two missing teens that they uncover the sadistic work of a prolific serial killer.

programme.title: Married With Secrets: Obsession Has Its Price
programme.desc: Bill Douglas is a respected doctor at Tufts University in Boston. But his focus isn't on science – it's on a bombshell prostitute he met in a red light district called the Combat Zone. Together, they lead double lives… leading to someone's murder.

programme.title: 'Til Death Do Us Part: Control, Kill, Delete
programme.desc: Matt and Niqué Leili's love story is a modern romance. They meet online, create a blended family, and appear to have it all. But on July 8, 2011, the illusion of a happy marriage is torn apart forever.

programme.title: 'Til Death Do Us Part: While You Were Sleeping
programme.desc: Dr. Mark Wangler and his wife Kathy appear to be living the middle-class dream: two sons, financial security, and a strong faith. But on September 4th, 2006, their façade falls down around them as a cold pre-meditated execution tears their family apart.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Ancient UFO Earth Landings
programme.desc: There's new evidence pointing to the possibility that ancient man was visited by extraterrestrials, influencing ancient technologies, architecture and art. One scholar reveals evidence supporting his theory that ancient Sumerians were created by aliens.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Deathbed Confessions
programme.desc: Deathbed confessions have provided a wealth of new evidence to UFO researchers from top intelligence, government and scientific witnesses who were sworn to secrecy in life, but couldn't leave this earth without revealing the truth.

programme.title: Chasing Conspiracies: The Alien Files
programme.desc: The final edition of the series examines a range of conspiracy theories relating to extra-terrestrials. There are those who believe that we have already made contact with aliens and that world governments are concealing this.

programme.desc: The idea of ancient astronauts goes back to Swiss author Erich von Daniken, whose books challenged religion and science. Find out who his enemies were, what his influences were, and why he was jailed.

programme.desc: With the recent discovery of thousands of earth-like planets in the universe the search for intelligent alien life continues. Some astronomers warn, when we find it, we should be prepared for advanced hostile extra-terrestrial species capable of anything.

programme.desc: George Rowe is a former criminal who goes undercover to bust a motorcycle gang, and the Masin brothers lead a resistance cell against Communist oppression in Czechoslovakia.

programme.title: Hardcore Heroes: Warriors on the Frontlines
programme.desc: During the Vietnam War, Drew Dix and a small handful of soldiers liberate an entire city from an enemy battalion. Russian soldier Viktor Leonov vanquishes German forces in the Arctic battlegrounds of World War 2.

programme.title: Deadliest Tech: Guns
programme.desc: The guns used in modern warfare are a terrifying fusion of unparalleled accuracy, range and stopping power. Take an inside look at the most ubiquitous and fundamental assets on the battlefield.

programme.title: Deadliest Tech: Explosives
programme.desc: Explosives are the terrifying sound and fury of modern warfare, annihilating any military target that threatens an army's personnel or stands in their way.

programme.title: Deadliest Tech: Helicopters

programme.title: Deadliest Tech: Fighters

programme.title: Bullet Points: Battle for the Pacific
programme.desc: See how America recovered from Pearl Harbor and, in one year, achieved supremacy in the Pacific over the Japanese empire. Featuring highlights of the defining battles that turned the tide in the Pacific theater and led to victory.

programme.title: Bullet Points: America's First War on Communism
programme.desc: Five years after the end of World War Two, the Cold War turns hot when America faces two communist giants in Korea and succeeds in keeping tyranny in check.

programme.title: Bullet Points: The Vietnam War

programme.desc: A family of seven calls on Tim McClellan to renovate their Cottonwood, AZ, home. He turns a storage area into a massive game room and builds a movable cocktail bar that reveals a 'secret' laundry room.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1928 Teacher's House
programme.desc: Tim is recruited by a Jerome, AZ, family to help restore their 90-year-old teacher's boarding house that has fallen into disrepair but retains much of its original detail. Tim creates a special piece of heirloom quality furniture to be cherished.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1930s Road Grader's House
programme.desc: Tim McClellan is enlisted to restore and modernize a one-of-a-kind river rock house built by the town's first road grader in Cottonwood, AZ. He uncovers some amazing historic details as he peels back the layers of the home's 1970s remodel.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1914 Dentist's House
programme.desc: Tim McClellan is recruited to revamp a couple's 100-year-old guest quarters in Jerome, AZ. Originally built for the town dentist, Tim cracks open the history books and rolls up his sleeves to transform the shabby suite into a historic treasure.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1915 Smelter House
programme.desc: Tim McClellan lends his talents to two amazing spaces that need reimagining in Clemenceau, Arizona. He creates wildly unique builds while unearthing the mysterious history of an adobe outbuilding.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1914 Middle Management House
programme.desc: Tim helps an overwhelmed young family restore their house in Jerome, AZ. Originally built for a copper mine manager, Tim uncovers high-end details and adds even more authentic features to the once illustrious home.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Barn Storming: A Wild Takedown in Pleasantville, Ohio
programme.desc: Mark and the guys have fought rain, snow and heat, but they've never had winds like these! The vicious Ohio winds turn a complicated job into a treacherous one as they attempt to save a giant double pen barn.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Beefy Bank-Barn Beams
programme.desc: Mark and the guys return to Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, where they stripped a massive bank barn earlier this year. This time, they are back for the incredible chestnut beams.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Restoring a Log Cabin in Pipe Stem, West Virginia
programme.desc: Mark and the guys restore an 1856 log cabin using wood from the original cabin, as well as wood from an old barn and a local saw mill. They team up with a local crew called, 'The Good Ole Boys,' to turn all this wood into one complete cabin.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Hitting the Barnwood Jackpot
programme.desc: Mark's client wants to use the barnwood from an enormous Pennsylvania bank barn to outfit his Arizona restaurant. It's a beast of a job in the middle of a heat wave, but the Barnwood Builders just won't quit.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Adapt and Overcome in Hamlin, WV
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders never give up, and despite some rough going early on, this one turns out to be worth all the extra effort. Mark goes on a cross-country search for replacement logs while the team pulls the cabin apart without sacrificing logs.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: With Garlic
programme.desc: Hosts dish on what their favourite dish that includes garlic are, from burgers to soup.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: On a Stick
programme.desc: Show hosts dish on their favourite foods on a stick, from fruit pops to cheesy tomatoes.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: For Brunch
programme.desc: The hosts discuss their favourite brunch foods, including sausages dipped in waffle batter, and delicious crab cakes.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Fried Chicken
programme.desc: Alton Brown, Claire Robinson, and more reveal where they get the best fried chicken.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Sweet and Savory
programme.desc: Scott Conant shows Michael his favourite burger and brew bar in an out-of-the-way corner of New Orleans, and then in Kansas City, Michael learns how two brother have stormed the classic barbecue scene with their award winning smoked meat.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Super Chefs' Favorite Foods
programme.desc: Earl Campbell and Michael dig into juicy brisket and warm peach cobbler at Texas' most famous barbecue restaurants.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Barbecue Boss of All Sandwiches
programme.desc: In Palm Springs, Calif., Michael Symon sinks his teeth into a meaty stacked sandwich and a beefy ballpark dog. In Oklahoma City, a gas station-turned-burger joint serves a fried apple and brie cheeseburger to fuel the soul.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Bacon Knows Best
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, Nev., Michael Symon takes down a towering fortress of succulent ribs and a sandwich that's a meaty trifecta of juicy brisket, pork and sausage.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Fire And Family
programme.desc: Roger Mooking heads to the South to visit two family-run barbecue joints that have been passing down recipes and rigs for generations.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Meat In Full Swing
programme.desc: Roger Mooking meets up with a few culinary titans in Tennessee who are swinging for the fences with outrageous rigs. At Wedge Oak Farm in Lebanon, Tenn., he joins Chef Trey Cioccia, owner of Nashville's Black Rabbit, to set up the Burn Tower.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Raising the Heat
programme.desc: Roger Mooking visits the Lone Star State, where they're taking open-fire cooking to new culinary heights with unique rigs. In Fort Worth, Texas, chef and restaurateur Lou Lambert invites Roger to his ranch to slow-roast whole hogs over oak coals.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Beef and Beyond
programme.desc: Beef is king in Texas, and Roger Mooking visits two pitmasters who are elevating traditional smoked meats with exciting global flavours. In Pearland, Texas, he helps Ronnie Killen season and smoke brisket and beef ribs to serve at Killen's Barbecue.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Denver
programme.desc: Andrew explores the frontier fare of Denver, Colorado. From classic game meats remade for the modern palette to the signature green chilli sauce of Den-Mex cuisine, Denver has become the region's cultural hub with an abundance of top-shelf food.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Cartagena
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern shows off the tempting flavors of Cartagena, Colombia. From fried fish and sizzling egg arepas to bedroom-boosting fruits and seafood stews, the taste of the coast infuses every bite.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Cape Cod
programme.desc: Andrew explores the treasures of Cape Cod's coastal cuisine. From fried clams to fresh-caught cod, clambakes to cranberry bog ice cream, the Massachusetts peninsula's culinary scene is cause for summertime celebration.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Key West
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern delights in the fun and funky flavors of Key West, Florida. From conch fritters, coconut pink shrimp and fried hogfish sandwiches to toasty Cubanos and quintessential Key lime pie, Key West is full of fresh eats and local ingredients.
